Details: Removal of aldicarb by a granular activated carbon filter was reported as 97 percent in a lab water [1489].  The GAC filter, Pentek model RFC20-BB, was a component of a point-of-entry Hyd-RO-Secure drinking water treatment system. The system consisted of a RO module, an advanced simultaneous oxidation process (ASOP) module, and the GAC filter. The GAC filter was tested following the ASOP module. The removal percentage reported was for GAC only. The GAC influent concentration was 160 μg/L with the pH level of 7.6. GAC properties were not reported.
